### https://www.linkedin.com/posts/sunnyliu_lessons-learned-from-building-genai-apps-activity-7440549765194379265-Imia
...
Lessons Learned from Building GenAI Apps (2023-Present)
...
After three years in the trenches moving from demos to production, here are the hard truths about developing with Generative AI.
...
LLMs are probabilistic, not deterministic. If your application cannot tolerate occasional hallucinations or mistakes (like a banking transaction or medical dosage calculator), GenAI is likely the wrong core technology. Use it for creative drafting or summarization, not precision execution.
...
2. Validate with a Panel
...
Never trust a single model’s output for critical decisions. Implementing multi-model cross-validation (asking two or more models the same question) significantly increases confidence in the result by flagging inconsistencies.
...
3. Keep the Human in the Loop
...
For applications requiring high security or strict quality control, automation is a trap. The human should always be the verifier. GenAI should draft and suggest, but a trained operator must approve and execute.
...
4. Narrow the Scope (AGI is a Myth)
...
Do not expect an LLM to "do everything." Treat it as a focused reasoning engine, not a general intelligence. Success comes from ruthlessly narrowing the scope and managing expectations. If the problem space is too broad, the system will fail.
...
5. RAG is Not Magic
...
Don't be fooled by demos of "chat with PDF." Retrieval-Augmented Generation quality degrades fast as your vector store scales. When dealing with massive document repositories (terabytes of data), retrieval latency plummets, chunks get lost, and context windows overflow. Chunking strategies break at scale.
...
6. Agents Have Their Own Problems
...
Agentic workflows can fix specific multi-step issues, but they introduce complexity, higher costs, and unpredictable loops. They are not a panacea. Sometimes a simple sequential chain is better than an autonomous agent.
...
The Golden Rule
...
Stop asking the LLM to do the hard thing. Do not use it for complex calculations or numeric challenges—it will fail.
...
Instead, ask the LLM to build you the tool to do it.
...
Need math? Ask it to write a Python script to run the calculation.
...
Need a complex workflow? Ask it to outline the steps for a human to follow.
...
Use the AI to generate the solution architecture, not the final numerical output. That is the only way to guarantee success.
...
I've been building with AI
...
daily for over a year now. Local models, agent
...
whole stack. It genuinely makes experienced people
...
• AI/ML Engineers building core intelligence
 • MLOps Engineers making models production-grade
 • AI Solutions Architects translating business into systems
 • AI Product Managers shaping real-world outcomes
 • AI Security & Ethics Specialists keeping trust intact
 • Domain specialists in NLP, CV, RL, Robotics, Speech
...
The shift is clear
...
AI has moved from experiments to infrastructure.

I’m excited to share that I’ve joined KKR as a Director, Enterprise Systems. This marks an important milestone in my journey, and I’m grateful for the opportunity to be part of an organization known for its global impact, strong values, and focus on innovation. I look forward to contributing to the firm’s technology vision—driving scalable, resilient platforms and enabling business growth through modern engineering and cloud-first strategies. A big thank you to my mentors, colleagues, and teams who’ve supported me along the way. Looking forward to learning, building, and creating meaningful impact in this new chapter. #NewBeginnings #Leadership #Technology #KKR #Gratitude

Architected, designed and developed the first end to end AI enabled modeling platform at American Express supporting risk, consumer marketing and commercial businesses with an estimated incremental billed business benefit of $5B. This was one of the first platforms to have adopted big data technology stack. Envisioned the core platform requirements for real time and stream-based analytics for the consumer marketing organization expected to bring in $5M incremental billed business benefits in 2020. Architected and designed an ML based intent classification model for multiple channel partners using structured spark streaming. Delivered and analytical capability that leverages closed loop customers spend data to gain insights about our customers and their engagement. The initiative is mainly to drive increase in revenue through personalisation and driving higher relevance in customer content marketing for Merchant campaigns. This is rolled out across 21 markets and 100+ partners. Built world-class information management practices through refined expertise in data management, governance, security, accessibility, tools and technology integrations. This enabled organizations to overhaul fragmented practices, drive automation, decommission legacy tech debt and driving fact-based decisions. Responsible for envisioning and creating a foundational enterprise data asset which could be leveraged across the company for both analytical and product use cases.

Model scoring to predict response, attrition, spend etc. using both machine learning and deep learning algorithm stack (KNN, ANN, GBM etc.) Deep learning algorithms for industry bonusing for the commercial business. Proactive line management, contextual hub, personalisation for marketing offers, dynamic intent classification using chat bots for multiple channels: Traveling cardmember, lounge capacity use cases for consumer marketing. Text and sentiment analysis Scoring and re-training of consumer marketing models. Led technology modernisation through reengineering, transformation and innovation. ### https://www.linkedin.com/posts/shikhatrivedi_datagovernance-dataleadership-dataquality-activity-7442563228364275712-X12x
...
Activity: Rishi
...
Stepping into conversations around Data Governance Leadership recently got me reflecting on what it truly takes to build a strong data foundation, especially in today’s fast-evolving, AI-driven world.
...
After exploring what a modern Data Governance Lead role demands, a few key themes stand out:
...
- Governance is no longer optional : 
It’s the backbone of every data-driven organization, ensuring trust, quality, and usability of data across the enterprise.

- From control to enablement : 
The role has evolved from enforcing rules to enabling smarter, faster decisions. Governance today is about making data accessible, without compromising security.
...
- Quality = Business Impact : 
Data quality isn’t just a technical metric. It directly influences customer experience, reporting accuracy, and strategic decisions.

- Tools are evolving, but mindset matters more
...
Platforms like Collibra and Azure Purview are powerful, but success depends on how well we align people, processes, and technology.
...
- Culture is everything : 
Building a culture of data stewardship, where everyone owns and respects data, is what truly scales governance.

- AI readiness starts with governance : 
Without trusted, well-governed data, even the most advanced AI initiatives will struggle.
...
For anyone working in or transitioning into data leadership roles, this space is becoming more strategic than ever.
...
Curious to hear from others:
...
- How is your organization evolving its data governance strategy?
- Are you seeing a shift toward federated or domain-driven models? #DataGovernance #DataLeadership #DataQuality #Collibra #AI #DataStrategy

results.title: KKR Launches Helix, a $10bn Platform for AI Infrastructure | MarketScreener UK
highlights:
KKR Launches Helix, a
...
# KKR Launches Helix, a $10bn Platform for AI Infrastructure
...
KKR has announced the formation of Helix Digital Infrastructure, a new venture dedicated to financing and developing the infrastructure required for artificial intelligence. Over $10bn in capital has already been committed by founding investors, including the Kuwait Investment Authority, Nvidia and power producer Vistra. The new entity will be led by Adam Selipsky, the former CEO of Amazon Web Services.
...
This initiative comes as AI-driven demand accelerates the construction of data centers and increases the need for energy and electronic components. Nvidia will contribute its expertise in designing specialized AI infrastructure, while Vistra will serve as the project's preferred electricity provider. KKR also plans to open Helix to other institutional investors once the initial commitments are finalized.The launch of Helix is part of a broader trend of private capital mobilization around artificial intelligence infrastructure. The rising cost of data centers and computing capacity is attracting numerous financial players, similar to Apollo and Blackstone, which recently announced a $35bn program linked to Anthropic projects. KKR noted that its infrastructure platform already manages over $100bn in assets, with a significant portion invested in energy and digital infrastructure.
...
KKR & Co. Inc. is a financial services group organized around 2 areas of activity: - insurance (67% of revenues): notably life insurance and pensions. The group also offers reinsurance solutions for private and institutional clients; - asset management (33%): management of private equity funds, equity funds, alternative credits, real estate assets, energy and infrastructure assets, investment funds, etc. In 2024, KKR & Co. Inc. has USD 637,572 million of assets under management.

### Megatron - Data driven object mapping
...
Data extraction from a legacy system is a dirty, evil job... so that's why we built Megatron. Leveraging an HSL engine, and creating a simple, XML based mapping language, we created a data extraction tool to convert legacy Fortran data into clean, modern XML.
...
### Mozart - System orchestration built on Apache Camel
...
Moving data around in a large- scale system can quickly become a nightmare. Leveraging Apache Camel, we built Mozart, a system orchestrator, to efficiently and transparently manage data flow in our systems.
...
### Shepherd - Distributed dynamic failover leveraging Zookeeper, for ordered data processing.
...
Often in financial systems, a particular client's stream of data must be processed in order. This order can only be guaranteed if the data stream is processed in a single process. However, if that process, or its environment, is subject to failure, then recovery and failover mechanisms become important. Shepherd is a Zookeeper-based system developed by my team to solve this problem.
...
### Snowflake - Redis-based, 64 bit monotonically increasing distributed counter
...
Assigning sequential trade IDs in a large-scale distributed system is a common problem in the financial world. Clients take their trade identifiers seriously, and want a simple number that increases in a deterministic fashion (i.e., not random). Leveraging Redis, we created a high- speed (100K/sec), 64-bit, monotonically increasing counter to solve this issue.

You will be responsible for designing and implementing AI-enabled solutions that are secure, resilient, compliant, and aligned with enterprise architecture standards. This role requires strong experience in translating business requirements into end-to-end AI solution architectures, including platform integration, governance, observability, and production readiness. - Documentation skills using PlantUML or similar.

# Data Scientist (AI Data & LLM Specialist)
...
Join the core team at Eclipse, where we’re building an AI agent-first marketplace that connects intelligence with real-world tasks, starting with data collection and labeling. We are seeking a Data Scientist to establish the foundation for how our data is labeled, processed, and prepared for consumption by next-generation Large Language Models (LLMs). Your work will be critical in transforming our raw data collections into valuable, AI-ready datasets.
...
- Proven experience as a Data Scientist or Machine Learning Engineer with a focus on data quality and preparation.
- Strong understanding of data labeling methodologies and hands-on experience with data annotation platforms and workflows.
...
- Demonstrated experience preparing datasets for training and fine-tuning Large Language Models (LLMs), including knowledge of techniques like tokenization, embeddings, and NER.
...
- Proficiency in Python and common data science libraries (e.g., Pandas, NumPy, Scikit-learn, spaCy, Hugging Face).
...
- Experience using APIs/SDKs to automate data annotation and active learning loops.
...
- Responsibilities
...
- Develop Data Labeling Strategies: Design and document a formal data annotation strategy, including clear, scalable, and efficient guidelines for labeling our data. Define and enforce quality metrics, including inter-annotator agreement.
...
- Optimize for LLM Consumption: Research, define, and prototype the optimal data formats, structures, and pre-processing steps required for fine-tuning and training LLMs on our datasets.
...
- Data Quality Analysis: Establish automated processes and metrics to analyze the quality of both raw and labeled data, providing feedback to improve our data collection and labeling workflows.
...
- Collaborate with Engineering: Work closely with the engineering team to guide the implementation of data processing pipelines and ensure the data infrastructure meets the needs of ML applications.
...
- Nice-to-Haves
- Experience with audio data processing and relevant libraries.
- Familiarity with data annotation platforms and tools.
- Knowledge of modern MLOps principles and practices.
- Experience with large language model data curation and Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) pipelines.

# Staff Software Engineer - AI/ML - Databricks

Published: 2026-07-01T00:18:44+00:00
Source: databricks.com (databricks.com)
Language: en

## Story

At Databricks, we are passionate about enabling data teams to solve the world's toughest problems — from making the next mode of transportation a reality to accelerating the development of medical breakthroughs. We do this by building and running the
